title: Show your real notes in the sidebar
summary: The list of notes in the left sidebar comes from sample data hard-coded into the app. This work makes the sidebar load each person's actual saved notes from the server, so they see their own writing the moment they open the app.
motivation: Source the sidebar notes list from the backend GET /notes/ listing API instead of in-memory mock data, so the left rail shows the user's real notes. Frontend-only; backend gaps (tags field, query params) are addressed in separate binders.

included: ui/src/features/notes/useNotes.ts — module-singleton composable: fetch, DTO→NoteMock adapter (ISO→epoch ms, tags=[]), loading/error refs
ui/src/features/shell/AppShell.vue — replace mockNotes seed with useNotes(), fetch on mount, seed activeId post-fetch
ui/src/features/shell/SidebarDrawer.vue — loading + error states in the note-list region (props + retry event)
update features/shell/__tests__/AppShell.test.ts and SidebarDrawer.test.ts to stub fetch with NoteResponse payloads
excluded: backend changes — no tags field on NoteResponse, no pagination, no search/filter query params (separate binder)
note create/update/delete wired to backend — list-only scope; createNote/deleteActiveNote keep mutating the local notes ref
pin-to-top feature from the mock — not in the stated scope; mock's NoteCard has a .card-pin button the current NoteCard.vue lacks, intentionally not added here
tag derivation client-side from content — filter UI stays in place but lights up empty until backend ships tags

consuming backend title field — frontend keeps deriving title via extractTitle(content) for parity with existing noteMockHelpers; backend title is ignored (flagged as a reconciliation note, not a bug)
SearchBox and TagFilter removal — they stay, operating client-side over fetched notes
adding the backend events SSE stream to the frontend — the /notes/events endpoint exists but wiring live updates is out of scope for a list-only slice

design_facts.stack: Vue 3 (<script setup> SFCs) + TypeScript + Vite; ofetch HTTP client; custom DTCG design-token system (Style Dictionary build); no router, no Pinia — module-singleton composables; auth gate in App.vue, navigation via ref toggles in AppShell.vue

work_items.title: Ask the server for your notes
work_items.summary: Add the small piece of code that asks the server for the list of a person's notes.
work_items.contract: Exports NoteDTO mirroring backend NoteResponse (backend/app/schemas/schemas.py:73): { id: string; title: string; content: string; createdAt: string; updatedAt: string } — ISO-8601 strings, NO tags field (backend gap). Exports listNotes(): Promise<NoteDTO[]> -> request<NoteDTO[]>("/notes/", { method: "GET" }). Reuses services/http.ts request() (bearer attach + single refresh-and-retry on 401); adds no HTTP machinery. Pattern mirrors services/auth.ts:16-21. The DTO intentionally keeps the backend's title field even though the frontend currently derives title via extractTitle — keeping it on the wire contract preserves the option to consume it later without another client change.

signals: contract-mutation:new-additive (first non-auth API client in services/ — establishes the notes API surface; every later notes feature builds on this)
capability-escalation:new-IO (first backend integration for notes; introduces network fetch where notes were previously in-memory mock data — a different failure mode than the existing auth fetches)

assertions: listNotes() issues GET to /api/notes/ with the bearer token from auth hooks
listNotes() resolves to the NoteResponse[] body unchanged (no field stripping, no renaming)
listNotes() rejects with ApiError carrying backend { detail } on non-2xx (delegates to http.ts error handling)
oracle.command: cd ui && npm run lint && npm run test -- src/services/notes.test.ts

work_items.title: Reshape server notes for the app
work_items.summary: Fetch the notes from the server and reshape them into the form the app already knows how to show, while tracking whether they are still loading or failed to load.
work_items.contract: Module-singleton composable mirroring features/auth/useAuth.ts pattern (module-level refs, useStorage optional, returned via useNotes()). Exposes: notes: Ref<NoteMock[]> (writable — AppShell keeps local create/delete mutations until a write-API binder lands), loading: Ref<boolean>, error: Ref<string|null>, fetchNotes(): Promise<void>. fetchNotes() calls listNotes(), maps each NoteDTO to NoteMock via an adapter: { id: dto.id, content: dto.content, tags: [], createdAt: Date.parse(dto.createdAt), updatedAt: Date.parse(dto.updatedAt) } — the adapter owns the ISO-string→epoch-ms conversion (backend ISO, frontend epoch) and the tags=[] placeholder (backend has no tags field; backend gap flagged in scope.excluded). On rejection, sets error to ApiError.detail and leaves notes unchanged. Does NOT discard the backend title field silently — it is unused by the adapter only because the frontend derives title via extractTitle(content); this is a recorded reconciliation decision, not a missing field.

signals: architectural-novelty:new-pattern-in-domain (first notes-domain module-singleton composable; mirrors useAuth but is new for notes — establishes the reactive-notes-store pattern every future notes feature will follow)
contract-mutation:new-additive (exposes the notes/loading/error refs AppShell and SidebarDrawer consume; the adapter is a new cross-shape boundary)

assertions: fetchNotes() populates notes with NoteMock[] mapped from NoteDTO: ISO strings → epoch ms via Date.parse, tags forced to []
fetchNotes() sets loading=true during the call, false after (both success and error paths)
on listNotes() rejection, error is set to the ApiError detail string and the notes ref is left unchanged
useNotes() returns the same refs across calls (module-singleton, not factory)
oracle.command: cd ui && npm run lint && npm run test -- src/features/notes/useNotes.test.ts

work_items.title: Load your notes when the app opens
work_items.summary: Make the app load the real notes when it opens and automatically select the most recently changed one.
work_items.contract: Replace `const notes = ref(mockNotes.slice())` (AppShell.vue:18) with `const { notes, loading, error, fetchNotes } = useNotes()`. Call fetchNotes() in onMounted; after resolve, seed activeId from the most-recently-updated fetched note (move the existing sort at AppShell.vue:20-22 into the post-fetch path — a watchEffect or a post-await assignment). Pass loading and error down to SidebarDrawer via new props. filteredNotes computed (AppShell.vue:48-67) stays unchanged — still client-side over the notes ref; SearchBox + TagFilter stay. createNote (AppShell.vue:85) and deleteActiveNote (AppShell.vue:146) keep mutating the local notes ref (write APIs are out of scope; they will be wired in a separate binder). Do NOT remove the mockNotes import if still used by tests — leave it for the test stub. The initial activeId must not be set synchronously from mockNotes anymore; it becomes null until fetchNotes resolves, then seeds.

signals: blast-radius:AppShell.vue owns many concerns (activeId, mode, search, tags, theme, create/delete, drawer); swapping the notes data source touches the core state wiring and the initial-activeId seeding logic

explicit-open-question:backend has no tags field — TagFilter renders empty and NoteCard tag chips are empty after this change; flagged as a backend gap in scope.excluded, to be addressed in a separate binder

assertions: AppShell mounts and renders notes fetched from GET /notes/ (no longer reads mockNotes at mount — mockNotes may still be imported only if tests use it as the stub source)
activeId is seeded to the most-recently-updated fetched note after fetchNotes resolves (not synchronously at construction)
SearchBox and TagFilter remain present in the rendered sidebar and operate client-side over fetched notes
createNote still adds a local note to the notes ref without a backend call (write API deferred)
oracle.command: cd ui && npm run lint && npm run test -- src/features/shell/__tests__/AppShell.test.ts

work_items.title: Show loading and error in the sidebar
work_items.summary: Show a gentle 'loading' message while notes are on their way, and if they fail to arrive, show what went wrong with a button to try again.
work_items.contract: SidebarDrawer.vue renders, inside .note-list (SidebarDrawer.vue:66), one of: (a) while loading — a non-blocking loading indicator (text like 'Loading…' or a small spinner, styled with var(--muted)); (b) on error with loading=false — an error line showing error plus a retry affordance (button calling a new retry event that AppShell wires to fetchNotes); (c) loaded, zero notes — the existing 'No notes match.' empty state (SidebarDrawer.vue:67-69) stays. Add props to SidebarDrawerProps (SidebarDrawer.vue:11-19): loading?: boolean (default false), error?: string|null (default null). Add emit: retry: []. Wire the retry button to emit('retry'). Loading indicator must not flash on fast fetches only if trivial — a simple conditional render is fine; no debouncing required. Styling uses existing design tokens (var(--muted), var(--accent), var(--surface-2)); no new tokens. The error retry path is the only way to recover from a failed fetch short of reloading the page.

signals: not-computed:contract (the loading/error prop shape and retry event are agreed in this item's contract and consumed by appshell-wire-fetch; no independent contract to evaluate)

assertions: SidebarDrawer shows a loading indicator inside .note-list when loading=true
SidebarDrawer shows an error line with a retry button when error is a non-empty string and loading=false
SidebarDrawer shows the normal note list (group-labels + NoteCards) when loading=false and error is null
clicking the retry button emits a retry event
oracle.command: cd ui && npm run lint && npm run test -- src/features/shell/__tests__/SidebarDrawer.test.ts

work_items.title: Feed tests notes shaped like the server's
work_items.summary: Update the automated tests so they pretend to be the server and feed in notes shaped the way the real server sends them.
work_items.contract: AppShell.test.ts: add a beforeEach that stubs global fetch to resolve GET /api/notes/ with mockNotes reshaped as NoteResponse[] — same id/content, add title (use extractTitle(content) or the literal H1), convert createdAt/updatedAt from epoch ms to ISO strings, drop tags. The two existing tag-filter assertions in AppShell.test.ts (the 'filters notes by search and tag' test at line 83 and the 'toggles a tag filter from the active note's meta row' test at line 102) cannot pass against backend-sourced notes because tags is forced to [] — remove those two tests with a TODO comment citing the backend tags gap (re-add when the tags field lands on NoteResponse). All other existing assertions (new note, edit mode, settings, markdown render, table/checkbox, copy/export/delete, drawer open/close, theme toggle) stay and must pass against the stubbed fetch. SidebarDrawer.test.ts: add cases for the loading and error props and the retry event. Do not weaken any assertion; the stubbed fetch is the only test seam.

assertions: beforeEach stubs fetch to resolve GET /api/notes/ with NoteResponse[] (id, title, content, ISO createdAt/updatedAt, no tags)
all existing non-tag assertions pass against the stubbed fetch (new note, edit/read, settings, markdown render, table/checkbox, copy/export/delete, drawer, theme)
the two tag-filter assertions are removed with a TODO comment citing the backend tags gap
SidebarDrawer.test.ts covers loading=true, error+retry, and loaded-empty states
oracle.command: cd ui && npm run lint && npm run test -- src/features/shell/__tests__/AppShell.test.ts src/features/shell/__tests__/SidebarDrawer.test.ts
